diagnosis.ai_diagnosis: Severe preeclampsia at 30 weeks gestation - hypertension (150/95), edema, and headache constitute the classic triad. This represents a critical shift from the initial bleeding presentation and requires immediate obstetric management.
ai_diagnosis_actions: Immediately assess for additional preeclampsia symptoms (visual disturbances, epigastric pain, hyperreflexia)
Obtain urgent laboratory studies: CBC, liver enzymes, creatinine, uric acid, protein/creatinine ratio
Initiate continuous fetal monitoring and assess fetal well-being
Administer antihypertensive therapy if diastolic BP >110 mmHg
Consider magnesium sulfate for seizure prophylaxis
Prepare for emergency delivery if maternal or fetal compromise develops
Immediate transfer to referral hospital with obstetric and neonatal intensive care capabilities
diagnosis.divergence_note: Clinician findings show preeclampsia symptoms (hypertension, edema, headache) at 30 weeks rather than the initially reported heavy bleeding and severe abdominal pain at 28 weeks. This represents a completely different obstetric emergency requiring different management protocols.

disclaimer: This is AI-generated decision support for a licensed clinician. It is not a diagnosis and must be reviewed before any action is taken.
